name: Balcony door pair
description: A pair of full-height glazed doors opening from an interior reception or principal bedroom onto a balcony, loggia, or shallow terrace. Read as a ceremonial threshold rather than a service exit: two narrow leaves, one full block wide each, meet on the centerline of a tall opening and swing outward symmetrically. The leaves are fully glazed top-to-bottom so they double as windows when shut, flooding the room with light from one whole side and framing the view beyond. The opening is typically set inside a tall arched or rectangular frame three to four blocks high, demanding ceiling clearance the rest of the wall lacks. An outer balcony, railing, or parapet platform must already exist on the exterior — these doors never open onto a sheer drop. Common in Renaissance palaces, French townhouses, and Mediterranean villas; rarer in modest dwellings.
